description: PHASE 2: Property & Unit Management - Backend CRUD API and Frontend pages. Covers TASK-007 and TASK-008.
steps: Create backend/app/models/property.py with Pydantic models: PropertyCreate, PropertyUpdate, PropertyResponse, UnitCreate, UnitUpdate, UnitResponse, UnitHistoryResponse

Create backend/app/services/property_service.py with CRUD business logic: list_properties (filter by city/status), create_property (owner only), get_property (with units count), update_property, soft_delete_property (409 if active contracts), list_units, create_unit, get_unit (with current contract info), update_unit, delete_unit (409 if occupied), get_unit_history

Create backend/app/routers/properties.py with all endpoints: GET/POST /properties, GET/PATCH/DELETE /properties/{id}, GET/POST /properties/{id}/units, GET/PATCH/DELETE /units/{id}, GET /units/{id}/history
Create frontend/src/types/property.ts with TypeScript interfaces for Property, Unit, PropertyFilter
Create frontend/src/api/properties.ts with all API functions using TanStack Query patterns
Create frontend/src/pages/properties/PropertyListPage.tsx with grid cards, filter bar (city, type, status), skeleton loading, empty state
Create frontend/src/pages/properties/PropertyDetailPage.tsx with property header, UnitGrid, tabs placeholder
Create frontend/src/components/property/PropertyCard.tsx showing name, address, occupied/total units, badge
Create frontend/src/components/property/UnitGrid.tsx with color-coded unit tiles (green=vacant, red=occupied, yellow=maintenance)
Create frontend/src/pages/units/UnitDetailPage.tsx with info, tabs for contract/invoice/maintenance
Add property routes to App.tsx router configuration
acceptance_criteria: Backend property/unit endpoints are registered and importable
Frontend builds with property list, detail, and unit pages
UnitGrid component renders colored tiles based on unit status
Filter functionality is wired up in PropertyListPage
verification: cd /projects/sandbox/PWA-Quan-Ly-Nha-Tro/backend && python -c "from app.routers.properties import router; print('Properties router OK')"
